tokens: Not very level for the gravimeter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know, but it's taken a couple better than this.
(Pause) Now, I've got to change film.
(Pause) Let me get things going here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I can get by this station without it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You've got 25 minutes at this station, guys.
We've given you somewhat of an extension here.
You're using up some of it back at the LM, but we've given you somewhat of an extension.
You've got 25 minutes at this station.
The primary priority will be subfloor documented samples, and then subfloor rake soil.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: As you get off, we'd also like to open the SEP and again get that to cool.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You wanted to turn it Off?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...the same thing we've been doing to it all aft(ernoon)...(correcting himself) all evening.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Leave it open and I'll dust it, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, the (SEP) temperature; they'd like to know.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You know, the thing I dread most?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I'm not going to be able to do much (about it) today, I don't think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, you know, we don't have nearly as much dust.
Yesterday we were wallowing around in it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and, Gene, if you're not off the Rover, how about the rest of the Rover readouts?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm off, but I'll get them for you.
I look at them, and they all look good to me.
And, you know, I keep forgetting to give them to you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I can finish the station, don't you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You know that SEP isn't getting much...Well, it's getting a little (dust) on it, but those mirrors don't clean off as nice as the LCRU mirrors.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This looks just like our old friend, the pyroxene gabbro, with the shiny ilmenite platelets in the vugs and partially recrystallized vesicles.
The textural variations are planar, and they're primarily - or, subplanar - in the concentrations of vesicles.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, I'm going to put this brush under my seat.
It's just getting too hard to get off that place up there (on the LCRU bracket).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Searching through the film supply under his seat) Bravo.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, this is certainly a uni(form) subfloor, (just) as we mapped it (pre-mission).
It's certainly a uniform rock type.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The only variations are those gray zones which just seem to be either finer or (have an) absence of vesicles.
Boy, I'm nose to nose with a piece of it right now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Say, Bob, where can I get a new set of bags?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Gene) Okay, you want new bags...They'll be under Jack's seat.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Having heard Gene, which he doesn't always do when Gene is talking to Parker) Under my seat, there's some, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Here I am, folks, in the middle of a boulder field.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know whether I mentioned it.
The mineral texture appears to be subophitic to...(sort of) like a good diabase, although a little coarser.
(Pause) But it's unquestionably organized (pause) with that variation in vesicle concentration.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, I've got to get new bags.
I've only got one left, and you don't have any, I don't believe.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Bob, I have the impression that these blocks are buried up here (and) that the mantle does exist, even on Camelot.
There are a few blocks that are lying out on the...(It) looks like they're lying more or less on the surface, but you can attribute those to craters that have disrupted the block field.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The big ones seem to be projecting out of the...(Hears Bob) The big ones seem to be projecting out of the mantle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do you see any such mantle on...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Although I can't see how the mantle in here could...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's not as...(Responding to Bob) No, I don't.
What's there seems to be what could have been knocked up there (by local impacts).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I see a place where I think we can skim some off the top of a rock, which I think we probably ought to do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But I don't have the impression of draping, so much as I have just of burial.
And I have a feeling that the zap-pitting process just has cleaned these boulders off of anything that may have been on top of them, in excess of what's around them, right now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Also, like Horatio, the...(Hears Bob) Go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You're talking about mantle...(correcting himself) (a sequence of) blocks, then mantle, and then cleaned off by zap pits, in other words.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Most of the rocks seem...That seems to be what has happened all over the Moon that we've looked at (during Apollo).
But the rocks are always cleaner than the surface, of course.

The far rim of Camelot...You can see...(In) fact, everywhere but where we are and on the rim near(est) the LM, the rim seems to be completely covered (with mantle) or, at least, the blocks don't show through.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They (the boulders) show up in the wall but not at the rim.
That's much like Horatio, but not to the extreme that we saw at Horatio.
I'd say, at Camelot, the mantle is - oh, maybe, at the most...The rim thickness, if that's mantle, is on the order of a half of what we saw at Horatio.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The pan should let you measure that...Well, we didn't get a pan at Horatio, but we got some Rover shots of it.
But you may be able to quantify that a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Turning toward the Rover) How you coming, Geno?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got everything cleaned up and Mark, gravimeter.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here's a nicely structured rock that we probably ought to work on here.
Structured, again, in the vesicle concentration.
And then I think we ought to try to get...Right over there, we can get mantle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, I'll tell you what impresses me about some of these rocks.
There's a lot of...They may be zap pits...I guess you looked at them closer than I did, but there sure is a lot of lineation in some of that white material, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, on a...on a...on a...on a visual-obvious scale.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If you don't...Let me see if it's up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The crystal grains seem to be linear, but they are more or less random.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's some rocks here that are...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...that are highly vesicular and there's others that are not.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gene, if this is what you mean, it's...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...you guys, that the primary priority is the blocks and then a rake soil of the white subfloor soil there.
And you've only got 15 minutes before we want you driving back to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me get these two first and then we'll go get that one, because there's two different kinds here...at least apparent kinds.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, I tell you, watch when you back up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We need to sample the structures, though, in this thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We need to get that stuff on the mantle, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we want to get an "around-the-corner" picture of one of those big ones, too.
See if we can get the structure of it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, can you hand me a bag, or I'll pick it up with a scoop, whichever you prefer.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's see if we can fix your (sample) bag thing tonight.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Okay, that looks like our old friend, the gabbro, all right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: 462 is Gene's fairly freshly fractured rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, you can put it in the bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, here's another one right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't squeeze these things (the tongs) anymore.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Is another of the same variety.
Wish we'd started on that structured rock because we're going to run out of time.
Let's go over there and get at least one off of it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What did you have picked out?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This in here with the layering in it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...looks like you'll be going in about 10 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) (To Gene) Why don't you get a flight line...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to get that from here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll come around from this end and go around to that side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'll go perpendicular to you more or less.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, that one right behind you is just vesicular, by comparison, to a high degree - like three times as much.
Oh, I hope those bags weren't in the way of every one of those pictures.
(Pause) Boy, I tell you there ought to be a lot of permanent shaded samples in here, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Just a little piece but that's...See...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about this chunk down there, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't think that'll...That plate (shaped) piece?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't think that'll come off very easy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That comes from 15 years as a trained hammer bearer.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: By golly, your geology training is coming in handy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You learn where to hit rocks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Won't all go in there but...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's all right, you can wrap it around it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get it...No, I'll get it, babe.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...if you could get that rake soil and maybe also get the soil off the top of one of those boulders that you thought you saw.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I've got to have Gene with me since (I) can't carry sample bags.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I probably can if I'm careful; but I keep dropping them.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Looking at the boulders they've just sampled) These rocks here have an awful lot...a much greater density of the white minerals in them - or crystals - than I've ever seen before, Jack.
Where did we see these kind before?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, when I looked at them right at first, that's what I thought.
But I think that the zap pits are making the white stand out more.
They're fooling you a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Because when I looked at it with the hand lens, it looked like a fairly normal gabbro, like some of those that have crystallized with the mare basalt.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You ready to take them back...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What I want is a sample of this soil off one of these rocks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, let's get that now and then let's get the rake sample.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But it looks to me like it's soil that's been thrown up there rather than...(Pause) This rock is about 3 meters in diameter, but it's one of the flat-surfaced rocks.
It only stands about - at the most - one-third of a meter high.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But we can get up about a meter from the soil/rock interface and get soil off the rock, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Whoops, oh, yeah; I got some soil.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pouring the soil) Okay, this is soil from a half a meter in.
It's about a centimeter deep and a half a meter in (from the outer edge of the top of the rock).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's take that chip there that's lying on top with the next scoop.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let's take the soil on that.
Put this...Before you pick that one up, pick that little chip up...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well...I don't want to get the chips.
Either that or a coherent rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pouring) I think we better leave it at that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Pick that other one up and I'll bag it real quick.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's the soil from on top the rock.
And we're taking a piece of the rock itself, which looks pretty much like the other one, Bob.
It might be a little bit more vesicular.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and that'll be in 466, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Here we are and I'll be able to grab it with my hand.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, the soil came from a half a meter in from the soil boundary.
We need to get a...Let me get over here and try to get one bag of soil that's away from the boulder.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to get my "after" while I'm here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Gene) Could you...(Hearing Bob) Could you...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the present time, we drop the rake soil, we'd just like to get the kilogram of soil somewhere between the boulders - (in) as open (a location) as you can.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob) Oh, you want a kilogram?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That'll replace the rake soil sample we were going to get.
And we'd like you moving in 3 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Joining Jack and responding to Bob) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) This will be a matched pair with our soil sample, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, bag 467 is where your kilogram is coming from.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) I'm sampling down to about 5 centimeters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Pinch her down tight or that will leak out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now let me get your big bag (SCB) tight.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me try to get a...(Pause) You got a shot of where my scoop was, didn't you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get an "after" of it, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Houston, we sampled about 3 meters southwest of the gnomon that was set up for the top-of-boulder soil sample.
So it's a match pair, really, in that regard.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Now I need to get a pan...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'll go over near the Rover and get one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) When do you want us to leave, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'd like you to leave immediately, if not sooner.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Singing) Hippity-hoppity, hippity-hoppity, hippity-hopping over hill and dale.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: My golly, this time goes fast!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and when you leave here...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...17, remember that we want to pick up...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And when we leave we want to take EP number 8 with us, guys.
We'd like the SEP turned back on and the blankets closed.
(Pause) Okay, Jack, I guess that's your option, you may...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...stop and take the charge off when you get to the distance or, if it's only a short one, you might like carrying it in your lap.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, I can hold it in my left arm on the seat.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you want the SEP on?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, both DSEA (Data Storage and Electronics Assembly) and the other switch.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, closed is...What happened to the Velcro on that other side?
(Pause) I thought they mounted that thing so that...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you (Houston) got the TG(E reading).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We made a mistake earlier and it's too late to rectify it in carrying these charges.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you don't have it off, we could stop and get off and get it, if you want to?
But this is a short distance.
You might want to carry this one.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I say the mistake was made earlier.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you turn this (SEP receiver) on?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's all on, all squared away.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Push that thing (probably the SEP cover) down and it'll stay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And how about a frame callout before you get back on, guys.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I need some new...(Responding to Bob) Do you want me to get it (a new magazine) here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Jack, it'd be my opinion, since you're just going back over the same path, that you came up this morning, it's probably not necessary.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I'll use it until it runs out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got a lot of film anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Gene) But when you leave me at...Okay.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, we'll let Gene take some of the photos near the bomb, or near the charge, perhaps.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Just jump up again and get your bottom...
You got to come this way quite a bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Your seat's rising with you, for some reason.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, the switch is coming On.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You know where we landed yet?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Obviously pleased with himself) I'm reading 085/1.4, and that's what my checklist said.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob; I guess my impression - and it's purely pure interpretation right at this stage - is that Camelot is mantled by whatever has formed the dark mantle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It does not seem to be mantled to the degree that Horatio is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt
